title: On macos after an intense network load, the forwarded ports into the podman container stop responding.
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

On macos after an intense network load, the forwarded ports into the podman container stop responding.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. start container with mapped ports `--network bridge -p 33333:33333 -p 33334:33334 -p 9634:9634 -p 9635:9635 -p 2001:2001 -p 2002:2002 -p 2003:2003 -p 2004:2004`

2. simulate high network activity

3. on the host machine curl comand fail to connect to forwarded ports. For example:
```
% curl "http://localhost:2001/service/?stat"
curl: (28) Failed to connect to localhost port 2001 after 26115 ms: Operation timed out
```
And of course I am checked that this curl command works inside container.


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
% podman --version
podman version 4.1.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
% brew list podman
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.1.1/bin/podman

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical Macbook with M1 Max

comments.body:
> simulate high network activity

Can you be more specific?


Any change you can build and test with gvproxy 0.4.0 (https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/releases/tag/v0.4.0)?
Maybe related https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12495

> Any change you can build and test with gvproxy 0.4.0 (https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/releases/tag/v0.4.0)? Unfortunately it not helped if I replaced gvproxy right. 1. I downloaded [gvproxy-darwin-arm64](https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/releases/download/v0.4.0/gvproxy-darwin-arm64) 2. I opened directory `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.1.1/libexec` and rename original gvproxy to gvproxy.old 3. I copied downloaded gvproxy-darwin-arm64 in place gvproxy and set right chown and chmod. 4. I restarded podman machine and reproduce described issue again.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I have the same issue with latest version of Podman on M1 MacBook Pro w/ macOS 13.2.

This issue seems to be fixed by gvproxy 0.5.0. I updated #12495 with details on issues with gvproxy 0.4.0  under heavy network load and the results of testing with 0.5.0.
Thanks, @binaryfields !  Closing given the issue seems fixed.
